quotes: His beard was shave as nigh as ever he can.

records.ety: [OE. shaven, schaven, AS. scafan, sceafan; akin to D. schaven, G. schaben, Icel. skafa, Sw. skafva, Dan. skave, Goth. scaban, Russ. kopate to dig, Gr. ska`ptein, and probably to L. scabere to scratch, to scrape. Cf. Scab, Shaft, Shape.]
records.morph: [imp. Shaved (shāvd);p. p. Shaved or Shaven (shāv"'n); p. pr. & vb. n. Shaving.]

defs: To cut or pare off from the surface of a body with a razor or other edged instrument; to cut off closely, as with a razor; as, to shave the beard.

To make bare or smooth by cutting off closely the surface, or surface covering, of; especially, to remove the hair from with a razor or other sharp instrument; to take off the beard or hair of; as, to shave the face or the crown of the head; he shaved himself.
quotes: I'll shave your crown for this.
The laborer with the bending scythe is seen Shaving the surface of the waving green.

defs: To cut off thin slices from; to cut in thin slices.
quotes: Plants bruised or shaven in leaf or root.

defs: To skim along or near the surface of; to pass close to, or touch lightly, in passing.
quotes: Now shaves with level wing the deep.

defs: To strip; to plunder; to fleece.
To use a razor for removing the beard; to cut closely; hence, to be hard and severe in a bargain; to practice extortion; to cheat.

records.ety: [AS. scafa, sceafa, a sort of knife. See Shave, v. t.]

defs: A cutting of the beard; the operation of shaving.
An exorbitant discount on a note.
A premium paid for an extension of the time of delivery or payment, or for the right to vary a stock contract in any particular.
A hand tool consisting of a sharp blade with a handle at each end; a drawing knife; a spokeshave.
The act of passing very near to, so as almost to graze; as, the bullet missed by a close shave.
